1454	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
1455	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
1456	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
1457	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
1458	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
1459	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1460	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
1461	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
1462	  ModuleCat.ofHom
1463	    (TensorProduct.lift
1464	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1465	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
1466	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
1467	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
1468	        (fun s φ φ' => by
1469	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
1470	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
1471	        (fun c s φ => by
1472	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
1473	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))

1493	    (restr X.unop N).map (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op

1507	noncomputable def internalHomEval
1508	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1509	    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) ⟶
1510	      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
1511	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
1512	    intro X Y f
1513	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
1514	    -- iter-224: the iter-222/223 `whnf` HEARTBEAT-BOMB diagnosis (the codomain `𝟙_` forcing

1517	    -- elementwise rewrite fires without any bomb; the six-step `evalLin`/`key`/`hdt` reduction below

1529	          (M.evalLin X φ s)

1533	      (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1534	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1535	      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s

1544	        (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq

1552	/-- **Precomposition `R(U)`-linear equivalence on dual sections induced by an iso `e : M ≅ M'`.**
1553	At an object `U`, sends a dual section `φ : M'|_U ⟶ R|_U` to `(e.hom|_U) ≫ φ : M|_U ⟶ R|_U`, with

1555	`globalSMul` (the `homModule` action), which commutes with this pre-composition, so the map is
1556	`R(U)`-linear. This is the section-level core of `dualIsoOfIso` (the dual is contravariantly
1557	functorial in isomorphisms). -/
1558	noncomputable def dualPrecompEquiv
1559	    {M M' : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} (e : M ≅ M') (U : D) :
1560	    letI := internalHomObjModule U M'
1561	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1562	    letI := internalHomObjModule U M
1563	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1564	    (restr U M' ⟶ restr U (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
1565	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op U) : Type u)]
1566	      (restr U M ⟶ restr U (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) := by
1567	  letI := internalHomObjModule U M'
1568	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1569	  letI := internalHomObjModule U M
1570	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1571	  exact
1572	    { toFun := fun φ =>
1573	        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1574	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ
1575	      invFun := fun ψ =>
1576	        (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1577	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ
1578	      map_add' := fun φ ψ => Preadditive.comp_add _ _ _ _ φ ψ
1579	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
1580	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1581	        exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
1582	      left_inv := fun φ => by
1583	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1584	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv

1586	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ) = φ
1587	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.inv_hom_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,

1590	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1591	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom
1592	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1593	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ) = ψ
1594	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,

1597	/-- **The presheaf dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism

1604	    {M M' : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} (e : M ≅ M') :
1605	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
1606	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk
1607	    (fun U => (dualPrecompEquiv e U.unop).toModuleIso)

1619	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1620	
1621	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1622	
1623	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1624	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1625	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1626	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1627	
1628	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1629	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1630	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1631	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1632	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1633	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1634	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1635	
1636	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1637	
1638	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1639	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1640	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1641	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1642	
1643	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1644	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1645	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1646	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1647	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1648	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1649	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1650	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1651	
1652	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1653	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1654	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1655	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1656	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1657	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1658	law is definitional. -/
1659	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1660	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1661	
1662	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
1663	
1664	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
1665	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
1666	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
1667	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
1668	
1669	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
1670	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
1671	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
1672	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
1673	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
1674	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
1675	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
1676	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
1677	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
1678	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
1679	
1680	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
1681	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
1682	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
1683	
1684	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
1685	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
1686	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
1687	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1688	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1689	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
1690	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1691	
1692	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
1693	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
1694	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
1695	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
1696	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
1697	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
1698	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
1699	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
1700	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1701	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
1702	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
1703	
1704	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1705	
1706	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1707	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1708	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1709	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1710	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1711	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1712	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1713	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1714	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1715	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1716	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1717	
1718	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1719	
1720	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1721	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1722	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1723	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1724	
1725	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1726	
1727	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1728	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1729	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1730	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1731	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1732	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1733	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1734	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1735	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1736	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso

1019	\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
1020	\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}
1021	
1022	\emph{Superseded route --- this entire section is off path and is not to be
1023	formalized.} The construction below realizes a \emph{full} symmetric monoidal
1024	category on all of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) by instantiating Mathlib's
1025	localization-of-monoidal-categories API. It was the original plan for supplying the
1026	associator. The relative Picard group law, however, is a structure on
1027	\emph{isomorphism classes} of invertible sheaves and consumes only the
1028	\emph{existence} of the associator, unitor and braiding isomorphisms
1029	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, built by hand as the scheme-theoretic
1030	analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\), not off a coherent monoidal
1031	category's \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\)). The associator
1032	is now obtained directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
1033	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
1034	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), which uses \emph{no} \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\),
1035	\emph{no} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), and \emph{no} stalk machinery. Accordingly
1036	the whiskering-stability lemmas and the stalk apparatus of this section are
1037	\emph{vestigial} and must not be re-attempted. The material is kept for the historical record only.
1038	
1039	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
1040	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
1041	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
1042	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
1043	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
1044	sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
1045	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
1046	whiskering-stability lemmas below.
1047	
1048	\paragraph{Two-tier strategy.} The group law is assembled by a \emph{two-tier}
1049	strategy, of which this route~(e) is the second tier. The \textbf{primary} tier
1050	builds the commutative group directly on isomorphism classes of \emph{locally
1051	trivial} (invertible) sheaves (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), mirroring
1052	Mathlib's ring-level Picard group \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) /
1053	\(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}). On top of
1054	the locally-trivial-scoped associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
1055	\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} (all
1056	either already proved or locally-trivial-scoped and assembled), it needs only the
1057	restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and the
1058	dual/inverse \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}; it uses \emph{no}
1059	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), \emph{no} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\), \emph{no}
1060	\(\mathtt{Skeleton}\), and in particular never invokes the stalk--tensor commutation
1061	(d.2). The \textbf{fallback} tier is the full \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to
1062	\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category on all of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)
1063	(this section); it is deferred because its load-bearing whiskering field
1064	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, for an arbitrary whisker object, needs
1065	(d.2) --- the stalk commuting with the relative tensor over a \emph{varying} ring, a
1066	Mathlib-absent ingredient --- and is retained only for the full
1067	monoidal generality on arbitrary modules, which the group law does not consume. The
1068	load-bearing local-injectivity half of \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) is thus the sole
1069	remaining open obligation of the \emph{fallback} tier only.
1070	
1071	\emph{Superseded route --- off path, not to be formalized.} The following lemma is
1072	the stalk-linearity packaging supporting the abandoned stalkwise proof of the
1073	whiskering-stability field (\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, FALLBACK).
1074	The group law never invokes stalks: the associator is built by direct gluing
1075	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}). This block
1076	is retained for the historical record only and must \emph{not} be formalized.
1077	
1078	\begin{lemma}
1079	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.
1080	
1081	[The $R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x$-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules]
1082	  \label{lem:stalk_linear_map}
1083	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1084	  Let \(X\) be a topological space, \(R\) a presheaf of commutative rings on
1085	  \(X\), and \(x \in X\) a point. For a morphism \(g : M \to N\) of presheaves of
1086	  \(R\)-modules, the induced map on stalks
1087	  \[
1088	    g_x \;\colon\; M_x \;\longrightarrow\; N_x
1089	  \]
1090	  is \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\)), and is
1091	  characterised on germs by
1092	  \(g_x(\mathrm{germ}_x\,s) = \mathrm{germ}_x(g_U\,s)\)
1093	  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_germ}\)). When the underlying map of
1094	  \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-valued stalks is an isomorphism, \(g_x\) is bijective
1095	  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_bijective\_of\_isIso}\)); in that case it bundles to
1096	  an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear equivalence
1097	  \(M_x \xrightarrow{\sim} N_x\) (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso}\)).
1098	
1099	  These four declarations are the \emph{(d.1)-partial implementation} --- the
1100	  linearity half of ingredient (d.1) of
1101	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} --- consumed by the
1102	  \(\mathrm{id} \otimes g_x\) step in the proof of that lemma. They build on the
1103	  stalk module structure of \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk};
1104	  the linearity packaging itself is project-original.
1105	\end{lemma}
1106	
1107	\begin{proof}
1108	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1109	  The stalk \(M_x = \mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) carries its
1110	  \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure from
1111	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk}. The underlying additive map
1112	  \(g_x\) on stalks is the filtered colimit of the section maps \(g_U\); since
1113	  each section map is \(R(U)\)-linear and the scalar action on the stalk is the
1114	  colimit of the section actions, \(g_x\) respects the
1115	  \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-action, which gives the linear map together with its
1116	  germ characterisation. Bijectivity is read off the underlying
1117	  \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-stalk isomorphism (a bijective linear map is a linear
1118	  equivalence), and bundling the inverse yields the linear equivalence.
1119	\end{proof}
1120	
1121	\emph{Superseded route --- off path, not to be formalized.} The following lemma is
1122	the load-bearing whiskering-stability field of the abandoned
1123	\((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) route to the
1124	associator (route~(e)). The group law on iso-classes consumes only the
1125	\emph{existence} of the associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), which
1126	is now built directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
1127	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
1128	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) with no whiskering and no stalks. This lemma ---
1129	including both its PRIMARY and FALLBACK proof routes and the stalk ingredients
1130	(d.1)/(d.2) below --- is therefore off the critical path. Retained for the
1131	historical record only.

1133	\begin{lemma}
1134	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.

1136	\leanok
1137	[Local injectivity of a whiskered localizer morphism (stalkwise, flatness-free)]
1138	  \label{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1139	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1140	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
1141	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally
1142	  bijective morphisms). Concretely the site carries the
1143	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) property for \(\mathtt{Ab}\), so \(J.W\) is
1144	  exactly the class of \(J\)-locally bijective morphisms; this is recorded as a
1145	  typeclass hypothesis \([J.\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\,\mathtt{Ab}]\) on
1146	  the statement. Let \(F\) be an \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of
1147	  \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of
1148	  \(R\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
1149	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R).\mathrm{map}\,g\) lies in \(J.W\). Then the
1150	  left-whiskered morphism
1151	  \[
1152	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
1153	  \]
1154	  is \emph{\(J\)-locally injective}. No flatness and no local-triviality
1155	  hypothesis on \(F\) is required.
1156	
1157	  This is the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\)-field content (the injectivity half) of the
1158	  instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), the load-bearing
1159	  field of the \emph{full-generality} route~(e) \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) path
1160	  (arbitrary \(F\)). For the relative Picard group law, however, the whisker object
1161	  \(F\) supplied by the sole consumer --- the locally-trivial-scoped associator
1162	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} --- is always \emph{locally trivial}
1163	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,F\)), and in that case local injectivity
1164	  of \(F \triangleleft g\) admits a markedly simpler, stalk-free proof, given as the
1165	  PRIMARY route below. The general stalkwise argument (ingredients (d.1)/(d.2)) is
1166	  retained only as the FALLBACK, required if the full route~(e) monoidal category on
1167	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) is later wanted; the locally-trivial
1168	  primary route already suffices for the group law.
1169	\end{lemma}
1170	
1171	\begin{proof}
1172	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:stalk_linear_map,
1173	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
1174	
1175	  \emph{PRIMARY route (\(F\) locally trivial --- the consumer's case; sheaf-level,
1176	  no stalks, no (d.2)).} Suppose \(F\) is locally trivial
1177	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,F\)), which is exactly the hypothesis its
1178	  sole consumer --- the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} --- supplies.
1179	  \(J\)-local injectivity of a morphism is \emph{local on a covering family}: it
1180	  suffices to produce a cover of \(X\) on each member of which \(F \triangleleft g\)
1181	  restricts to a locally injective morphism. Take a trivialising open cover
1182	  \(\{V\}\) of \(X\) for \(F\), so that \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) on each \(V\).
1183	  On such a \(V\):
1184	  \begin{itemize}
1185	    \item the substrate--restriction compatibility \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1186	      gives a canonical isomorphism
1187	      \[
1188	        (F \triangleleft g)\big|_V \;\cong\; (F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V)
1189	      \]
1190	      --- restriction along the open immersion \(V \hookrightarrow X\) commutes with
1191	      \(\otimes\), and the whiskering \(F \triangleleft g = \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g\)
1192	      is \(\otimes\) with an identity, so it restricts to \(\mathrm{id}_{F|_V}
1193	      \otimes (g|_V) = (F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V)\);
1194	    \item the trivialisation \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) together with the left
1195	      unitor \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_V \otimes_V (-) \cong
1196	      (-)\)) identifies \((F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V) \cong g|_V\).
1197	  \end{itemize}
1198	  Composing, \((F \triangleleft g)|_V\) is isomorphic to \(g|_V\). Now \(g \in J.W\),
1199	  and membership in \(J.W\) (local bijectivity) is stable under restriction along an
1200	  open immersion, so \(g|_V \in J.W\); in particular \(g|_V\) is \(J\)-locally
1201	  injective. An isomorphism preserves local injectivity, hence \((F \triangleleft
1202	  g)|_V\) is locally injective on \(V\). As the \(\{V\}\) cover \(X\) and local
1203	  injectivity is local on the cover, \(F \triangleleft g\) is \(J\)-locally
1204	  injective. \emph{No stalks and no (d.2) stalk--tensor commutation enter}; the
1205	  single comparison lemma \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (a presheaf-level
1206	  comparison, decomposed into its two open-immersion base-change halves) carries the
1207	  argument, and thereby moves \emph{onto} the critical path.
1208	
1209	  This is \emph{not} the section-level route that tensors a bare section map \(g(V)\)
1210	  --- only injective --- and stalls on a \(\mathrm{Tor}_1\)/flatness obligation. Here
1211	  the argument is sheaf-level and uses that \(g\) is locally \emph{bijective} (a
1212	  \(J.W\) morphism), reduced through the unitor to \(g|_V\) (again a \(J.W\)
1213	  morphism); a mere injection is never tensored, so no flatness is needed.
1214	
1215	  \emph{FALLBACK route (arbitrary \(F\); stalkwise, required only for the
1216	  full-generality route~(e) \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) path).} For an
1217	  \emph{arbitrary} (not necessarily locally trivial) \(F\), local injectivity is
1218	  proved stalkwise, by the argument Mathlib uses for fixed-base presheaves in
1219	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\)
1220	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}), ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). A
1221	  morphism in \(J.W\) is, on the topological site of \(X\), a \emph{stalkwise
1222	  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
1223	  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
1224	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving
1225	  \[
1226	    (F \triangleleft g)_x
1227	      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes_{R_x} g_x
1228	      \;\colon\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} M_x \;\longrightarrow\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} N_x .
1229	  \]
1230	  Tensoring an isomorphism \(g_x\) by the identity \(\mathrm{id}_{F_x}\) yields an
1231	  isomorphism --- no flatness is needed, since \(g_x\) is invertible, not merely
1232	  injective. Hence \(F \triangleleft g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, in particular
1233	  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).
1234	
1235	  The stalkwise computation rests on three ingredients with distinct status at the
1236	  pinned commit:
1237	  \begin{enumerate}
1238	    \item[(d.1-done)] the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear stalk-map packaging
1239	      (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\) and its companions, \cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}):
1240	      a morphism of presheaves of modules induces an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear
1241	      map on stalks, characterised on germs, that becomes a linear equivalence once
1242	      the underlying \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-stalk map is an isomorphism. This is built
1243	      project-side, axiom-clean, and furnishes the \(\mathrm{id} \otimes g_x\) step
1244	      above.
1245	    \item[(d.1-bridge)] (remaining) the site-\(J.W\) \(\Longleftrightarrow\)
1246	      stalkwise-iso characterisation on \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\): that a morphism lies
1247	      in \(J.W\) iff its stalk maps are isomorphisms at every point. The concrete
1248	      Mathlib assembly route uses the \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) structure
1249	      together with the topological stalk criteria --- local injectivity
1250	      \(\Longleftrightarrow\) injectivity on stalks and local surjectivity
1251	      \(\Longleftrightarrow\) surjectivity on stalks --- bridging the site-level
1252	      \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective}\) / \(\mathtt{IsLocallySurjective}\)
1253	      to the \(\mathtt{TopCat.Presheaf}\) stalk versions. This half is still
1254	      Mathlib-absent at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level.
1255	    \item[(d.2)] (remaining) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
1256	      \otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
1257	      module-presheaf tensor, under which \((F \triangleleft g)_x\) is identified
1258	      with \(\mathrm{lTensor}\,F_x\,(g_x) = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) (using
1259	      that \(\mathtt{tensorLeft}\) / \(\mathtt{tensorRight}\) preserve filtered
1260	      colimits over a module category). This is genuinely Mathlib-absent over a
1261	      \emph{varying} ring and is the largest remaining piece.
1262	  \end{enumerate}
1263	  Mathlib supplies the analogues for presheaves valued in a fixed monoidal concrete
1264	  category, but not for modules over a varying ring; porting (d.1-bridge) and (d.2)
1265	  to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) is the remaining work. Once (d.2) lands, the
1266	  conclusion is immediate and flatness-free: by
1267	  \(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap\_bijective\_of\_isIso}\) the stalk map \(g_x\) is a
1268	  linear equivalence, and the linear-equivalence \(\mathrm{lTensor}\) by \(F_x\)
1269	  carries it to an isomorphism, finishing the proof.
1270	
1271	  This stalkwise fallback is required \emph{only} if the full-generality
1272	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) route~(e) --- the
1273	  monoidal category on \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), for an
1274	  arbitrary whisker object \(F\) --- is later wanted. For the relative Picard group
1275	  law the whisker objects are locally trivial, and the PRIMARY route above suffices,
1276	  trading the Mathlib-absent (d.2) for the single comparison

1288	\begin{lemma}
1289	% SUPERSEDED route (route-(d)/(e) whiskering/stalk machinery); pending deletion once the assoc re-route (morphism-level descent) lands. Declaration still present and still backing the current `tensorObj_assoc_iso` proof.
1290	[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
1291	  \label{lem:whisker_of_W}
1292	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1293	  With notation as in \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: for an
1294	  \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of \(R\)-modules \(F\) and a morphism \(g\) whose
1295	  underlying additive-presheaf map lies in \(J.W\), both whiskerings again lie in
1296	  \(J.W\):
1297	  \[
1298	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; F \triangleleft g \in J.W,
1299	    \qquad
1300	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; g \triangleright F \in J.W .
1301	  \]
1302	  These two statements are precisely the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) and
1303	  \(\mathtt{whiskerRight}\) data required by the class
1304	  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) for
1305	  the morphism property \(J.W\); together they furnish the instance
1306	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) of \cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}. No flatness and no
1307	  local triviality is used --- they are the flatness-free replacements for the
1308	  flat variants of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}.
1309	\end{lemma}
1310	
1311	\begin{proof}
1312	  \uses{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1313	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of \(J.W\), it suffices to show
1314	  \(F \triangleleft g\) is both locally injective and locally surjective.
1315	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is free and needs no hypothesis on \(F\): the presheaf
1316	  tensor is right exact, so \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the locally-zero cokernel
1317	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\), which is therefore locally
1318	  zero (Mathlib \(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)). \emph{Local
1319	  injectivity} is \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. The two together
1320	  give \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
1321	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
1322	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf monoidal structure, which is an isomorphism
1323	  and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\).
1324	\end{proof}

1336	\begin{lemma}

1338	[The \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal substrate (route~(e) target)]
1339	  \label{lem:jw_ismonoidal}
1340	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:whisker_of_W}
1341	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The sheafification localizer \(J.W\) on
1342	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) is \emph{monoidal}: it carries an
1343	  instance
1344	  \[
1345	    J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal},
1346	  \]
1347	  whose two fields are the whiskering-stability lemmas \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}
1348	  (and which is multiplicative because sheafification inverts identities and
1349	  composites). Consequently, by Mathlib's
1350	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)
1351	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) applied to the already-monoidal category
1352	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\), the localization functor
1353	  \(L = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) (which is the localization at
1354	  \(J.W\)), and the unit isomorphism \(L(\mathbf{1}) \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), the
1355	  category
1356	  \[
1357	    \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
1358	      \;=\; \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}
1359	  \]
1360	  acquires a full \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure whose tensor is the substrate
1361	  \(\otimes_X\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) and whose associator,
1362	  left/right unitors, braiding and \emph{all} coherence (pentagon, triangle,
1363	  naturalities) are derived by the API. This is the route~(e) target: the
1364	  associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors
1365	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso} are
1366	  then components of this derived monoidal structure rather than hand-assembled
1367	  composites.
1368	
1369	  This block states the route~(e) construction; it depends on the sole open obligation
1370	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. It mirrors Mathlib's
1371	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\) construction one categorical level up.
1372	\end{lemma}
1373	
1374	\begin{proof}
1375	  \uses{lem:whisker_of_W}
1376	  Multiplicativity of \(J.W\) (closure under identities and composition) is part
1377	  of its being a localizer (\(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). The two
1378	  whiskering-stability fields of \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) are
1379	  exactly the left- and right-whiskering statements of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.
1380	  Hence \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) holds. Sheafification of presheaves of
1381	  modules is a localization at \(J.W\) (the underlying identification of
1382	  sheafification with localization at the locally-bijective class), and the unit
1383	  presheaf sheafifies to \(\mathcal{O}_X\); feeding these to
1384	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) produces the \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
1385	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with all coherence derived, as in
1386	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}. No \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) structure enters
1387	  at any point.
1388	\end{proof}

1392	\leanok
1393	[Associator for $\otimes_X$ on locally trivial objects]
1394	  \label{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
1395	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso}
1396	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1397	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
1398	  \(M, N, P \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). There is an isomorphism
1399	  \[
1400	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
1401	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1402	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
1403	  \]
1404	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), natural in \(M, N, P\). The group law on
1405	  \(\otimes\)-iso-classes (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only
1406	  the \emph{existence} of this isomorphism, the proposition
1407	  \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \cong M \otimes_X (N
1408	  \otimes_X P)\bigr)\); no pentagon, triangle, or naturality coherence of it is
1409	  ever required.
1410	
1411	  The current Lean pin additionally carries \emph{locally trivial}
1412	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\), \cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial})
1413	  hypotheses on \(M, N, P\). The construction below produces the isomorphism for
1414	  arbitrary \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules; the hypotheses are retained only to match
1415	  the carrier the group law multiplies on, and are not used in the gluing.
1416	\end{lemma}
1417	
1418	\begin{proof}
1419	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1420	  \textbf{Status (route mismatch, deferred).} The \emph{current} realization of this
1421	  associator uses the route-(d) whiskering composite
1422	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W,lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} and the sheafification
1423	  bridge \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}); through
1424	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} --- whose left-whiskering component is
1425	  itself unproved --- it transitively depends on an open proof obligation and is
1426	  therefore \emph{not} axiom-clean. The gluing route described below is the
1427	  \emph{intended} obligation-free realization, but discharging it requires
1428	  \emph{morphism-level descent for \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)}: gluing a family of
1429	  local isomorphisms, agreeing on overlaps, into a single global isomorphism of
1430	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. That is the same descent-infrastructure family
1431	  the \(\otimes\)-inverse needs (\cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}); accordingly, the
1432	  associator re-route \emph{and} the deletion of the vestigial whiskering/stalk
1433	  apparatus are deferred jointly with the dual block, and are not available with the
1434	  current infrastructure. The gluing description below records the target realization.
1435	
1436	  The isomorphism is built by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through the
1437	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, with no
1438	  recourse to any whiskering or stalk apparatus. Fix a
1439	  common open cover \(\{U\}\) of \(X\). On each member \(U\) form the canonical
1440	  composite
1441	  \[
1442	    \bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P\bigr)\big|_U
1443	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1444	    \bigl(M|_U \otimes_U N|_U\bigr) \otimes_U P|_U
1445	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1446	    M|_U \otimes_U \bigl(N|_U \otimes_U P|_U\bigr)
1447	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1448	    \bigl(M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\big|_U ,
1449	  \]
1450	  in which:
1451	  \begin{itemize}
1452	    \item the first arrow is two applications of the restriction-compatibility
1453	      isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} along the open immersion
1454	      \(U \hookrightarrow X\): first \((M \otimes_X N)|_U \cong M|_U \otimes_U
1455	      N|_U\) tensored with \(P|_U\), then \(\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X
1456	      P\bigr)|_U \cong (M \otimes_X N)|_U \otimes_U P|_U\);
1457	    \item the middle arrow is the \emph{canonical presheaf-level associator}
1458	      \(\alpha\) of the (symmetric) monoidal category
1459	      \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_U\)
1460	      (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct.associator}\),
1461	      \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}(i)), read on the restricted modules;
1462	    \item the third arrow is again two applications of
1463	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, in the opposite direction.
1464	  \end{itemize}
1465	  Each of the three arrows is a \emph{canonical} isomorphism: the
1466	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is induced
1467	  by the open immersion with no choices, hence natural in its module arguments, and
1468	  the presheaf associator \(\alpha\) is a fixed natural transformation. Therefore,
1469	  on a double overlap \(U \cap U'\), restricting the \(U\)-composite and the
1470	  \(U'\)-composite to \(U \cap U'\) yields the same morphism: each of the three
1471	  arrows commutes with the further restriction to \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1472	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the presheaf associator, so the two local
1473	  composites agree on the overlap.
1474	
1475	  Because the internal hom \(\mathcal{H}om(-,-)\) of sheaves of
1476	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is itself a sheaf on \(X\), a family of morphisms defined
1477	  on the members of an open cover and agreeing on the overlaps glues to a single
1478	  global morphism. Applying this both to the local composites above and to their
1479	  local inverses produces a global morphism
1480	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \to M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\) whose restriction
1481	  to each \(U\) is the local composite, together with a two-sided inverse glued from
1482	  the local inverses; hence the glued morphism is a global isomorphism --- the
1483	  associator. The single non-formal input of this re-route is the
1484	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}; the presheaf
1485	  associator (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}(i)) and the sheaf-of-modules
1486	  internal-hom gluing are already available.
1487	
1488	  Note that, unlike the pointwise-existence statement
1489	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} --- a \emph{proposition} proved
1490	  cover-pointwise with \emph{no} overlap obligation --- the associator is
1491	  \emph{global data}, a single isomorphism of sheaves on all of \(X\). Assembling it
1492	  from local pieces is therefore genuinely stronger than that pattern: the
1493	  overlap-naturality compatibility of the local composites must be discharged before
1494	  gluing, and is discharged above. The locally-trivial hypotheses of the statement
1495	  are not consumed; the comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is applied to the
1496	  global, untrivialised restrictions \((M \otimes_X N)|_U\), so no free-cover
1497	  specialisation can replace it. The vestigial
1498	  whiskering / \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / stalk apparatus
1499	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W,lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W,lem:jw_ismonoidal}) plays
1500	  no part in this proof and is superseded. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
1501	  consumes only the \emph{existence} of the resulting isomorphism, never its pentagon,
1502	  triangle, or naturality coherence.
1503	\end{proof}

1559	\leanok
1560	[Inverse of an invertible module]
1561	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
1562	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
1563	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
1564	        lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1565	  % NOTE: ON the critical path under the PRIMARY locally-trivial group law (it
1566	  % supplies inverses to lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup, in whose uses it sits).
1567	  % Only under the route-(e) fallback is it dispensable: there the group law uses
1568	  % the tensor-invertibility predicate IsInvertible (def:scheme_modules_isinvertible),

1577	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1578	  % "\begin{lemma}
1579	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}
1580	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1581	  % ...
1582	  % \item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
1583	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map

1588	  % \begin{definition}
1589	  % \label{definition-pic}
1590	  % Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space.
1591	  % The {\it Picard group} $\Pic(X)$ of $X$ is the

1594	  % corresponding to tensor product.

1596	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1597	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction
1598	  is an isomorphism) and Definition~\texttt{definition-pic} (the Picard group is
1599	  the abelian group of iso-classes of invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules under
1600	  tensor product).}
1601	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
1602	  bundle. The dual
1603	  \[
1604	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1605	  \]
1606	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
1607	  isomorphism
1608	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
1609	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
1610	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
1611	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
1612	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1613	\end{lemma}
1614	
1615	\begin{proof}
1616	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
1617	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}
1618	  \textbf{Infrastructure-blocked.} This statement is \emph{not} realizable with the
1619	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module infrastructure presently available: its construction
1620	  depends on a \emph{sheaf internal hom of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules}
1621	  \(\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\), an object that does not yet
1622	  exist at the presheaf, sheaf, or general-categorical level (there is no
1623	  monoidal-closed / internal-hom / dual structure on
1624	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) or \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\), and no object-level
1625	  descent assembling a global \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module from local pieces). The dual
1626	  object \(L^{-1}\) therefore cannot even be \emph{named} until that infrastructure
1627	  is built. The construction of the required internal hom, its dual, and its
1628	  evaluation is decomposed into individually-formalizable sub-steps in
1629	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra} (\cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom} through
1630	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}); the present lemma is realized once those land,
1631	  and its Lean body is a placeholder until then.
1632	
1633	  We record the intended mathematical route, all three steps of which are correct
1634	  modulo the missing object. \emph{The route is as follows.} Take
1635	  \(L^{-1} := \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\) to be the
1636	  sheaf-level dual of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}, and let
1637	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) be the evaluation of
1638	  \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}.
1639	
1640	  \emph{Step 1 (\(L^{-1}\) is a line bundle).} Local triviality of \(L\)
1641	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\)) furnishes an open cover \(\{U\}\) of
1642	  \(X\) with trivialisations \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The internal hom commutes
1643	  with restriction along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow X\), so on each \(U\)
1644	  \[
1645	    L^{-1}\big|_U \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(L|_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1646	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1647	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U ,
1648	  \]
1649	  the last identification being the evaluation-at-\(1\) isomorphism for the dual of a
1650	  free rank-one module. Hence \(L^{-1}\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an
1651	  object of \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
1652	
1653	  \emph{Step 2 (the contraction is a local isomorphism).} The evaluation/contraction
1654	  morphism
1655	  \[
1656	    \varepsilon_L \;\colon\; L \otimes_X L^{-1}
1657	      \;=\; L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1658	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_X,
1659	    \qquad s \otimes \phi \longmapsto \phi(s),
1660	  \]
1661	  is a morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Restricting to a trivialising
1662	  open \(U\), the restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1663	  commutes \(\otimes_X\) past \((-)|_U\):
1664	  \[
1665	    (L \otimes_X L^{-1})\big|_U \;\cong\; L|_U \otimes_U L^{-1}|_U
1666	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U ,
1667	  \]
1668	  using the trivialisations of Step~1. Under these identifications \(\varepsilon_L|_U\)
1669	  becomes the contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\) of
1670	  the free rank-one module with its dual, which is precisely the left unitor
1671	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\)) and hence an
1672	  isomorphism. Thus \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) is an isomorphism on each member of the cover.
1673	
1674	  \emph{Step 3 (glue to a global isomorphism).} The local trivialisations are induced
1675	  by the open immersions with no choices, so the local contraction isomorphisms
1676	  \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) agree on the overlaps \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1677	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the unitor under further restriction. As
1678	  ``being an isomorphism'' is local on \(X\) --- a morphism of sheaves of
1679	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose restriction to every member of an open cover is an
1680	  isomorphism is itself an isomorphism --- the globally-defined \(\varepsilon_L\) is an
1681	  isomorphism \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\). Equivalently, the
1682	  local inverses glue (they agree on overlaps for the same reason) to a two-sided
1683	  inverse of \(\varepsilon_L\).
1684	
1685	  Consequently, \emph{once \(L^{-1}\) and \(\varepsilon_L\) are available from
1686	  \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}}, every line bundle \(L\) has the two-sided tensor
1687	  inverse \(L^{-1}\) under \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes of
1688	  line bundles is an abelian group --- the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1689	  The local-triviality of \(L^{-1}\) (Step~1) is \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} and
1690	  the local-iso\(\Rightarrow\)global-iso passage (Step~3) is the already-closed
1691	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}; the single non-available input is the dual object
1692	  and its evaluation of \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}.
1693	\end{proof}

1745	  % \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules (Stacks \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} /
1746	  % \texttt{lemma-pullback-invertible}), independently of the open-immersion
1747	  % restriction-compatibility iso.

1749	  functor
1750	  \[
1751	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
1752	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
1753	  \]
1754	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
1755	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
1756	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
1757	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
1758	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
1759	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
1760	  canonical isomorphism

1767	\begin{proof}
1768	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
1769	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
1770	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
1771	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
1772	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
1773	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
1774	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
1775	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\): on each
1776	  affine open where \(\mathcal{N}\) and \(\mathcal{N}'\) are free of rank one
1777	  over \(A\), both sides evaluate to the free rank-one \(B\)-module, so
1778	  the comparison is an isomorphism. No flatness of \(A \to B\) is required
1779	  at this level, as the modules involved are locally free. The compatibility
1780	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the

1798	of \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}) and the tensor-inverse lemma

1800	\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) are on the critical path.

1931	  \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\)).} The commutative group is built directly on
1932	  isomorphism classes of \emph{locally-trivial} (equivalently \(\otimes\)-invertible)
1933	  sheaves, mirroring Mathlib's ring-level Picard group
1934	  \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) / \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\)
1935	  (\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}). The group operation is
1936	  \([L]\cdot[M] := [L \otimes_X M]\), well-defined on iso-classes by the
1937	  bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_X\)
1938	  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}) and again locally trivial by
1939	  the closure lemma \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}; the identity is
1940	  \([\mathcal{O}_X]\); and the inverse of \([L]\) is the class of the dual
1941	  \([L^{-1}]\) supplied by \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} (with
1942	  \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)).

1949	    \quad

1951	    \quad
1952	    \mathtt{mul\_assoc} \;\leftarrow\; \langle \alpha_{X,Y,Z} \rangle,
1953	    \quad
1954	    \mathtt{mul\_comm} \;\leftarrow\; \langle \beta_{X,Y} \rangle,
1955	  \]

1957	  the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, and commutativity is the braiding

1880	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.

1883	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
1884	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
1885	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
1886	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
1887	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
1888	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
1889	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
1890	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
1891	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
1892	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
1893	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
1894	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
1895	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
1896	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
1897	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
1898	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
1899	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
1900	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
1901	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
1902	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
1903	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
1904	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
1905	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
1906	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
1907	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
1908	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
1909	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
1910	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
1911	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

1914	
1915	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
1916	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
1917	
1918	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
1919	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
1920	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
1921	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
1922	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
1923	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
1924	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).

1926	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.

1941	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
1942	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
1943	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
1944	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
1945	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
1946	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
1947	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
1948	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
1949	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
1950	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
1951	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
1952	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
1953	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
1954	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
1955	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
1956	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
1957	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.

1959	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
1960	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
1961	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
1962	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
1963	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
1964	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
1965	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
1966	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
1967	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_

1969	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
1970	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
1971	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
1972	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
1973	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
1974	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
1975	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the

1977	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
1978	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅

1981	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the

1987	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }

1992	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
1993	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))

2033	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by

2059	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
2060	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
2061	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
2062	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
2063	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
2064	    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
2065	    IsIso φ := by
2066	  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
2067	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
2068	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
2069	    intro x
2070	    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
2071	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
2072	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
2073	      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
2074	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
2075	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
2076	    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
2077	        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
2078	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
2079	    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.

2081	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
2082	      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
2083	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
2084	    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
2085	  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
2086	  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
2087	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
2088	  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩

2090	  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS

2264	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site
2265	equivalence (the SHARED root of both ⊗-inverse bridges)
2266	
2267	For a topological space `X` and an open `U : Opens X`, Mathlib supplies the
2268	1-categorical equivalence `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
2269	(`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`) but leaves, as a documented `## TODO`, the
2270	upgrade of this to an *equivalence of sheaf categories*
2271	
2272	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.

2274	This is the single Mathlib-absent root on which BOTH remaining ⊗-inverse bridges
2275	(the A-engine `homOfLocalCompat` gluing and the C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
2276	reduce: re-evaluating a slice-internal-hom along the open immersion `U ↪ X` is

2281	the dense-subsite instance `(overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite …`. The sole

2299	-- identifier `Opens` resolves to the scheme-theoretic `Scheme.Opens` (expecting a
2300	-- `Scheme`), shadowing the intended point-set `TopologicalSpace.Opens`. We therefore
2301	-- fully qualify every `TopologicalSpace.Opens …` here; `Over`/`Sieve`/`GrothendieckTopology`
2302	-- are `CategoryTheory` names with no scheme shadow and stay unqualified.
2303	
2304	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
2305	
2306	/-- Pointwise cover-correspondence underlying `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`: a
2307	sieve `S` on `W : Opens ↥U` is a covering sieve in the subspace topology iff its
2308	pushforward along the open-embedding image functor `↥U ↪ X` is a covering sieve in
2309	`X`. Both sides are the pointwise neighbourhood-cover condition of
2310	`Opens.grothendieckTopology`, matched across the injection `Subtype.val`. -/
2311	private lemma overEquiv_image_cover_iff (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥U) (S : Sieve W) :
2312	    (S.functorPushforward ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U))
2313	        ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
2314	          (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W)
2315	      ↔ S ∈ (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) W := by
2316	  constructor
2317	  · intro h y hy
2318	    have hx : (y : X) ∈
2319	        (((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W) :=
2320	      ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
2321	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, hxV⟩ := h y hx
2322	    obtain ⟨W', a, b, hSa, _⟩ := hf
2323	    refine ⟨W', a, hSa, ?_⟩
2324	    have hyim : (y : X) ∈
2325	        ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W' := b.le hxV
2326	    obtain ⟨z, hz, hzeq⟩ := hyim
2327	    rw [← (Subtype.ext hzeq : z = y)]; exact hz
2328	  · intro h x hx
2329	    obtain ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩ := hx
2330	    obtain ⟨W', a, hSa, hyW'⟩ := h y hy
2331	    refine ⟨((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).obj W',
2332	      ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).map a, ?_,
2333	      ⟨y, hyW', rfl⟩⟩
2334	    exact ⟨W', a, 𝟙 _, hSa, by simp⟩
2335	
2336	/-- The inverse of `overEquivalence U` exhibits `(Opens ↥U, subspace topology)` as a

2338	`Equivalence.sheafCongr` consumes; the only non-formal field is the pointwise
2339	cover-correspondence `overEquiv_image_cover_iff`. -/
2340	instance overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite :
2341	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
2342	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
2343	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
2344	  functorPushforward_mem_iff {W S} := by
2345	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
2346	    rw [show Sieve.overEquiv ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W)
2347	          (S.functorPushforward (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse)
2348	        = S.functorPushforward
2349	            ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U) by
2350	      rw [Sieve.functorPushforward_comp]; rfl]
2351	    exact overEquiv_image_cover_iff U W S

2353	/-- **The open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site equivalence.** For a topological space
2354	`X`, an open `U : Opens X`, and any value category `A`, the slice sheaf category
2355	over `U` is equivalent to the sheaf category on the open subspace `↥U`:
2356	
2357	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
2358	
2359	This completes the documented Mathlib `## TODO` at `Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` and
2360	is the SHARED root both ⊗-inverse bridges (`homOfLocalCompat`, `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
2361	reduce to. Built by transporting `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U` across
2362	`CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, using the project-local dense-subsite
2363	instance `overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite`.
2364	
2365	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
2366	noncomputable def overSliceSheafEquiv (A : Type w) [Category.{v} A] :
2367	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌
2368	      Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
2369	  (TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
2370	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
2371	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A

2719	  \(\varphi_i : M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) with \(\varphi_i|_{U_i \cap U_j} =
2720	  \varphi_j|_{U_i \cap U_j}\), section-wise gluing in the sheaf \(N\) produces, for

2726	  of \(N\). This is exactly the sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\), so it is a

2756	  analogue of \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}: it is to the internal hom
2757	  / dual exactly what that lemma is to the tensor product. It is the \emph{H2$'$
2758	  ingredient} of the restriction-compatibility isomorphism of the C-bridge
2759	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} --- the ring-iso/dual commutation that, composed with
2760	  the open-immersion pushforward comparison H1, identifies the dual of a restriction
2761	  with the restriction of the dual.
2762	\end{lemma}
2763	
2764	\begin{proof}
2765	  The two assignments \(\varphi \mapsto e^{-1} \circ \varphi\) and
2766	  \(\psi \mapsto e \circ \psi\) are mutually inverse, since \(e \circ e^{-1} =
2767	  \mathrm{id}_S\) and \(e^{-1} \circ e = \mathrm{id}_R\) as ring homomorphisms, so the
2768	  two composites are the identity on functionals. Each assignment is \(R\)-linear:
2769	  additivity is immediate, and for a scalar \(r \in R\) the defining relation
2770	  \(r \cdot m = e(r) \cdot m\) of the pulled-back module structure makes each linearity
2771	  check a single rewrite --- \(e^{-1}\) (resp.\ \(e\)) intertwines the \(R\)-action on
2772	  the source with the \(R\)-action on the target precisely because \(e\) and \(e^{-1}\)
2773	  are inverse ring homomorphisms identifying \(R\) with \(S\). The forward map is
2774	  therefore an \(R\)-linear bijection, i.e.\ an \(R\)-linear equivalence; packaging the
2775	  two one-sided maps with the inverse identities gives
2776	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}\).
2777	\end{proof}

2779	\begin{definition}
2780	
2781	\leanok
2782	[Precomposition equivalence on dual sections]
2783	  \label{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}
2784	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dualPrecompEquiv}
2785	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual}
2786	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings and let \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\)
2787	  be an isomorphism of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. On a fixed open \(U\), precomposition
2788	  with \(e\) gives an \(R(U)\)-linear equivalence between the dual sections
2789	  \[
2790	    \mathtt{dualPrecompEquiv}\,e\,U \;\colon\;
2791	      \bigl(M'|_U \to R|_U\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \bigl(M|_U \to R|_U\bigr),
2792	      \qquad \varphi \;\longmapsto\; \varphi \circ e|_U,
2793	  \]
2794	  with inverse \(\psi \mapsto \psi \circ (e^{-1})|_U\). This is the section-level core of
2795	  the contravariant functoriality of the presheaf dual \(M \mapsto M^\vee\)
2796	  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual}) in isomorphisms: precomposing a functional on \(M'\) with
2797	  the comparison isomorphism produces a functional on \(M\), \(R(U)\)-linearly and
2798	  invertibly.
2799	\end{definition}

2801	\begin{definition}
2802	
2803	\leanok
2804	[The presheaf dual carries an iso \(M \cong M'\) to an iso \(M'^\vee \cong M^\vee\)]
2805	  \label{def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso}
2806	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso}
2807	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}

2809	  canonical isomorphism of presheaf duals
2810	  \[
2811	    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e \;\colon\; M'^\vee \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M^\vee
2812	  \]
2813	  --- note the reversal of direction, the dual being contravariant --- assembled
2814	  open-by-open from the precomposition equivalences
2815	  \(\mathtt{dualPrecompEquiv}\,e\,U\) of \cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}.
2816	  Naturality in \(U\), i.e.\ compatibility of these sectionwise equivalences with
2817	  restriction, is the routine check that precomposition by \(e\) commutes with
2818	  restricting functionals.

2843	\begin{lemma}
2844	
2845	\leanok
2846	[Open-immersion slice-site sheaf equivalence (the shared slice bridge)]
2847	  \label{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
2848	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overSliceSheafEquiv}
2849	  % SOURCE: Mathlib code-location provenance (this is NOT an external literature
2850	  % citation, so it carries no verbatim mathematical SOURCE QUOTE). This lemma
2851	  % completes the documented TODO at Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean, lines
2852	  % 19--22, reproduced here for orientation:
2853	  %   "show that both functors of the equivalence overEquivalence U are continuous
2854	  %    and induce an equivalence between Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A
2855	  %    and Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A for any category A."
2856	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(U \subseteq X\) an open subset, with associated open
2857	  immersion \(\iota : U \hookrightarrow X\). The reindexing equivalence
2858	  \(\mathtt{overEquivalence}\,U : \mathtt{Over}\,U \simeq \mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U}\)
2859	  --- between the slice of \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) over \(U\) and the opens of the subspace
2860	  \(\widetilde{U}\) --- upgrades to an \emph{equivalence of sheaf categories}
2861	  \[
2862	    \mathtt{Sheaf}\bigl((\mathtt{Opens.grothendieckTopology}\,X).\mathtt{over}\,U\bigr)\,A
2863	      \;\;\simeq\;\;
2864	    \mathtt{Sheaf}\bigl(\mathtt{Opens.grothendieckTopology}\,U\bigr)\,A
2865	  \]
2866	  for any target category \(A\), compatible with the module-pullback comparison
2867	  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). This completes the Mathlib TODO recorded at
2868	  \texttt{Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean} (lines 19--22). It is the \emph{shared root}
2869	  underlying both the restriction-compatibility isomorphism for the dual
2870	  (\cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}) and the morphism-descent / gluing engine
2871	  (\cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}): both reduce to identifying a
2872	  slice-indexed family of sections over \(\mathtt{Over}\,U\) with an
2873	  open-immersion-indexed family over \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U}\), and that
2874	  identification is exactly this equivalence.
2875	\end{lemma}
2876	
2877	\begin{proof}
2878	  The functor \(\mathtt{overEquivalence}\,U\) realises \(\mathtt{Over}\,U\) as a
2879	  \emph{dense subsite} of \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U}\): every open of the subspace
2880	  \(\widetilde{U}\) is covered by down-sets \(\{V : V \le U\}\), and these down-sets are
2881	  precisely the objects of the slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,U\). One therefore applies the
2882	  dense-subsite transfer \(\mathtt{Functor.IsDenseSubsite.sheafEquiv}\): a continuous,
2883	  cocontinuous, dense inclusion of sites induces an equivalence of the associated sheaf
2884	  categories. The continuity and cover-lifting hypotheses are supplied by the slice-site
2885	  instances of \(\mathtt{Sites/Over}\) (the Grothendieck topology
2886	  \(J.\mathtt{over}\,U\) and its comparison with the topology pulled back along
2887	  \(\iota\)).
2888	
2889	  The over-category coherence that is laborious for a general site --- the pseudofunctor
2890	  identities \(\mathtt{Over.mapId}\), \(\mathtt{Over.mapComp}\) and the associativity
2891	  \(\mathtt{overMapPullback\_assoc}\) --- is here \emph{automatic}: because
2892	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) is a thin poset, every hom-set is a subsingleton, so each such
2893	  square commutes by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) (and its heterogeneous form
2894	  \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.helim}\) where source and target objects differ only up to a
2895	  propositional equality of opens). The sole sectionwise content is the down-set
2896	  identification
2897	  \[
2898	    \iota_{!}\bigl(\iota^{-1}(V)\bigr) \;=\; V
2899	    \qquad (V \le U),
2900	  \]
2901	  i.e.\ the image under the open immersion of the preimage of an open contained in \(U\)
2902	  is that open again. Transporting sections along this equality and invoking the
2903	  dense-subsite equivalence yields the stated sheaf equivalence, compatible with
2904	  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) by the same thinness argument.
2905	\end{proof}
