2907	\begin{lemma}
2908	[The dual of a line bundle is a line bundle]
2909	  \label{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}
2910	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_isLocallyTrivial}
2911	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2912	        lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
2913	  % NOTE (iter-228, EMPIRICALLY confirmed via lean_goal): the "verbatim mirror"
2914	  % proof sketch below is INCORRECT past Step 3 / H1. Steps 1--3 + H1 of the
2915	  % mirror DO typecheck for the dual; the residual after H1 is exactly
2916	  %   (pushforward beta).obj (dual A)  ~=  dual ((pushforward beta).obj A),
2917	  % i.e. open-immersion pushforward commutes with the presheaf dual. This does
2918	  % NOT close via H2' / restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv as claimed: the tensor's
2919	  % H2 worked because tensorObj is SECTIONWISE M(U) (x)_{R(U)} N(U) (a literal
2920	  % restrictScalars-image, so the strong-monoidal tensorator applies), whereas
2921	  % dual = internalHom(-, 1) is the SLICE internal hom -- its value is a
2922	  % morphism-module over Over U, NOT a sectionwise restrictScalars-image -- so it
2923	  % has NO sectionwise strong-monoidal analogue. restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv
2924	  % is only the ModuleCat-level shadow and cannot be lifted the way the tensor's
2925	  % was. The slice/Over-reindexing the sketch dismisses as "a structural
2926	  % reindexing, not a stalk" IS the hard kernel: closing the residual needs the
2927	  % open-immersion slice-site equivalence (Over V in Opens Y ~= Over (f''V) in
2928	  % Opens X, transporting morphism-modules naturally) -- a Mathlib-absent
2929	  % ~150--300 LOC build, NOT discharged by restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv.
2930	  % This route is still d.2-free, but it is NOT the cheap verbatim mirror the
2931	  % sketch describes. See task_results/.../TensorObjSubstrate.lean.md (iter-228).
2932	  % CORRECTION (now reflected in the proof body below): the residual is discharged
2933	  % by the shared slice-site sheaf equivalence
2934	  % lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv (the open-immersion <-> slice transport on
2935	  % the thin poset Opens X), NOT by restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv -- which survives
2936	  % only as the ModuleCat-level sectionwise shadow inside that transport.
2937	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
2938	  % modules), Lemma~lemma-constructions-invertible (item~2)
2939	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200--L4213).
2940	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2941	  % "\item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
2942	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
2943	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
2944	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
2945	  % is an isomorphism."
2946	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR,
2947	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible} (item~2).}
2948	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(L \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with
2949	  \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\). Then the sheaf-level dual
2950	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} is again locally trivial of
2951	  rank one, i.e.\ \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\).
2952	\end{lemma}
2953	
2954	\begin{proof}
2955	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso,
2956	        lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
2957	  Local triviality of \(L\) furnishes, around each point, an open \(U\) with a
2958	  trivialisation \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The crux is the
2959	  restriction-compatibility isomorphism
2960	  \[
2961	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)\big|_f \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(L|_f\bigr)
2962	  \]
2963	  for an open immersion \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\). Its first three steps run exactly
2964	  as for the closed linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, because both
2965	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) and the tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) are of the form
2966	  \(\mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{obj}\) of a presheaf:
2967	  \begin{enumerate}
2968	    \item reduce restriction to the abstract pullback,
2969	      \((-)|_f \cong (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via
2970	      \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\);
2971	    \item move the pullback inside the sheafification,
2972	      \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \cong
2973	      \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\), via
2974	      \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\);
2975	    \item strip the outer sheafification with \(\mathtt{.mapIso}\), reducing the claim
2976	      to the presheaf-level comparison.
2977	  \end{enumerate}
2978	  These three steps, together with the open-immersion pushforward comparison \emph{H1},
2979	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) (the identification of
2980	  two left adjoints of the same pushforward, \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\)
2981	  together with \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\)), are reused \emph{verbatim} from
2982	  the tensor and do typecheck for the dual.
2983	
2984	  Here the parallel with the tensor \emph{ends}. After H1 the residual to close is
2985	  \[
2986	    (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,A)
2987	      \;\cong\;
2988	    \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,A\bigr),
2989	  \]
2990	  i.e.\ the open-immersion pushforward commutes with the presheaf dual. For the tensor
2991	  the corresponding residual closed through the ring-iso commutation, because
2992	  \(M \otimes_X N\) is \emph{sectionwise} \(M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)\) --- a literal
2993	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\)-image, to which the strong-monoidal comparison applies.
2994	  The dual is \emph{not} of this form: \(\mathtt{dual} = \mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\)
2995	  is the \emph{slice} internal hom, whose value at an open \(V\) is a module of
2996	  morphisms over the slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,V\), not a sectionwise
2997	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\)-image. Consequently the ring-iso/dual commutation
2998	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv} is only the \emph{ModuleCat-level shadow}
2999	  of the comparison; it does \emph{not} by itself discharge the residual, and the naive
3000	  ``verbatim mirror'' \emph{H2$'$} of the tensor's ring-iso step does not close it.
3001	
3002	  The residual is instead discharged by the shared slice-site sheaf equivalence
3003	  \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}. Under that equivalence the slice internal
3004	  hom computed over \(\mathtt{Over}\,V\) inside \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) corresponds to the
3005	  slice internal hom computed over \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U}\); because
3006	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) is a thin poset, the comparison is precisely the poset-thin
3007	  reindexing of \(\mathtt{overEquivalence}\,U\), all of whose naturality squares commute
3008	  by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). The ring-iso/dual commutation
3009	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv} survives only as the sectionwise algebraic
3010	  shadow carried along inside this transport. Combining the equivalence with the
3011	  trivialisation gives
3012	  \[
3013	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U
3014	      \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(L|_U\bigr)
3015	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
3016	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U,
3017	  \]
3018	  the last identification being evaluation at \(1\), the canonical iso of the dual of a
3019	  free rank-one module with itself. Hence \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) is locally trivial of
3020	  rank one.
3021	
3022	  No tensor stalk, and no whiskering \(M \triangleleft \eta\) of the sheafification
3023	  unit, is ever invoked: the comparison reduces to the open-immersion
3024	  \(\leftrightarrow\) slice transport of \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
3025	  (a structural reindexing on a thin poset, not a stalk). (Equivalently, this is item~2
3026	  of Stacks Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible}, tag 01CR: the dual of an
3027	  invertible module is invertible.)
3028	\end{proof}

3030	\begin{lemma}
3031	
3032	\leanok
3033	[Local isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global isomorphism]
3034	  \label{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}
3035	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict}
3036	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi \colon M \to N\) a morphism in
3037	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\).
3038	  Suppose that for every point \(x \in X\) there is an open neighbourhood \(U_x\) of
3039	  \(x\) such that the restriction of \(\varphi\) to \(U_x\) --- the image
3040	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) under the
3041	  restriction functor along the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\colon U_x \to X\) ---
3042	  is an isomorphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_x\). Then \(\varphi\) is an
3043	  isomorphism.
3044	\end{lemma}
3045	
3046	\begin{proof}
3047	  Being an isomorphism for a morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules can be
3048	  checked on the underlying morphism of sheaves of abelian groups, and there it can be
3049	  checked stalkwise. Fix a point \(x \in X\). Choose a preimage \(x' \in U_x\) of \(x\)
3050	  under the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\). By hypothesis the restriction
3051	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) is an
3052	  isomorphism, hence so is its image under any functor --- in particular under the
3053	  stalk-at-\(x'\) functor on the underlying ab-sheaves. The natural isomorphism
3054	  ``restriction commutes with stalks'' (\(\mathtt{restrictStalkNatIso}\)) identifies
3055	  this with the stalk of (the underlying ab-sheaf morphism of) \(\varphi\) at the image
3056	  point \(x\); transporting along it shows that stalk is an isomorphism. As this holds
3057	  at every \(x\), and a morphism of sheaves of abelian groups all of whose stalks are
3058	  isomorphisms is itself an isomorphism (stalkwise iso-detection,
3059	  \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_stalkFunctor\_map\_iso}\) on \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\)), the
3060	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism of \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism. Finally the forgetful
3061	  functor \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules.toPresheaf}\) reflects isomorphisms, so \(\varphi\)
3062	  itself is an isomorphism.
3063	\end{proof}

3065	\begin{definition}
3066	
3067	
3068	\leanok
3069	[Scheme-level linearity wrapper \(\mathtt{homMk}\) (A-bridge step (ii))]
3070	  \label{def:scheme_modules_homMk}
3071	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homMk}
3072	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Given a morphism
3073	  \(g \colon M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf} \to N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf}\) of
3074	  the underlying presheaves of abelian groups, together with a hypothesis \(hg\) that
3075	  \(g\) is \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear sectionwise, \(\mathtt{homMk}\,g\,hg\) is the
3076	  morphism \(M \to N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) that this data determines. It
3077	  is a thin wrapper that packages \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.homMk}\) at the scheme
3078	  level: the sectionwise-linearity datum promotes the ab-group morphism to a genuine
3079	  morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Its \(\mathtt{@[simp]}\) companion
3080	  \(\mathtt{toPresheaf\_map\_homMk}\) records that forgetting back recovers \(g\), i.e.\
3081	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,X).\mathtt{map}\,(\mathtt{homMk}\,g\,hg) = g\). This is step
3082	  (ii) of the A-bridge \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}: once the
3083	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism has been glued, \(\mathtt{homMk}\) re-attaches the
3084	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linearity to produce the global morphism of modules.

3086	
3087	\begin{lemma}
3088	[Compatible local morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global morphism]
3089	  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
3090	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
3091	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
3092	  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
3093	  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
3094	  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
3095	  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
3096	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
3097	\end{lemma}
3098	
3099	\begin{proof}
3100	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_homMk, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
3101	  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
3102	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
3103	  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.
3104	
3105	  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
3106	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups. The morphisms-between-them presheaf
3107	  \(\mathcal{H} := \mathtt{presheafHom}\,(M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\,
3108	  (N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\), whose value at an open \(W\) is the abelian
3109	  group of morphisms \(M|_W \to N|_W\) of presheaves of abelian groups, is itself a
3110	  \emph{sheaf of types} --- this is the ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
3111	  \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\), which consumes the sheaf condition of \(N\).
3112	  Regarding \(\mathcal{H}\) as a \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\), its universal gluing
3113	  property \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) amalgamates any compatible family of local
3114	  sections of \(\mathcal{H}\) into a unique global section. This route is preferred over
3115	  the raw sieve-level amalgamation \(\mathtt{presheafHom\_isSheafFor}\), which carries
3116	  strictly more bookkeeping.

## Red flags

### ⚠️ CRITICAL — Blueprint proof sketch falsified by iter-230 probe: `lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial`

**Location**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, lines 2954–3028, `\begin{proof}` for `lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial`

**The claim**: After Steps 1–3 + H1 (which typecheck verbatim from the tensor, as correctly noted), the proof body states (lines 3002–3010):

> "The residual is instead discharged by the shared slice-site sheaf equivalence `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. Under that equivalence the slice internal hom computed over `Over V` inside `Opens X` corresponds to the slice internal hom computed over `Opens U~`; because `Opens X` is a thin poset, the comparison is precisely the poset-thin reindexing of `overEquivalence U`…"

**Why this is wrong**: The iter-230 probe empirically verified (Lean file, lines 2118–2162, section "iter-230 C-wiring diagnostic") that after Steps 1–3 + H1, the residual lives at the **PresheafOfModules level** — specifically:

```
(PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)
    ≅  PresheafOfModules.dual ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val)
```

`overSliceSheafEquiv` has type:
```
Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
```

This is a **Sheaf-category** equivalence (fixed value-category `A`). The residual is a **PresheafOfModules** isomorphism over a **varying ring**. Three distinct mismatches make `overSliceSheafEquiv` inapplicable here:

1. **Level mismatch**: Step 3 already stripped the outer sheafification (via `mapIso`), leaving a purely presheaf-level goal. `overSliceSheafEquiv` operates on sheaves, not presheaves of modules.
2. **Value-category mismatch**: `overSliceSheafEquiv` is parametric in a fixed value-category `A`. The dual's value module `internalHomObjModule` at `V` is `Hom(M|_V ⟶ R|_V)`, which carries an `R(V)`-module structure over the **varying** ring — not a fixed-A Sheaf object.
3. **Slice granularity mismatch**: The dual uses the per-open-`V` slice `restr V = pushforward₀ (Over.forget V)`, a fine per-point slicing. `overSliceSheafEquiv` works over the whole-`U` slice site `(gt X).over U`, which does not see this per-`V` structure.

**Consequence**: A prover following the blueprint proof sketch for `lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial` would attempt to apply `overSliceSheafEquiv` to close the post-H1 presheaf residual and find it categorically impossible — the types don't align and no coercion bridges them. The Lean file documents precisely this outcome (OUTCOME (ii): "This residual CANNOT be discharged by the shared root `overSliceSheafEquiv`").

**What the blueprint should say instead**: The `% NOTE` block at lines 2913–2936 already records the historical correction. The actual proof body has not been updated to match what OUTCOME (ii) shows is actually needed — a new presheaf+module-level slice comparison:
```
Hom_{Over_X(fV)}(restr(fV) A, restr(fV) 𝟙_X)  ≅  Hom_{Over_Y V}(restr V ((pushforward β)A), restr V 𝟙_Y)
```
natural in V and R_Y(V)-linear, induced by the slice equivalence `Over_Y V ≌ Over_X(fV)` (the per-V shadow of `Opens.overEquivalence`, valid because `f.opensFunctor` is fully faithful). This is a genuine new build, **not** reducible to the existing Sheaf-level `overSliceSheafEquiv`.

- `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` at line 691: body is `:= sorry`. Blueprint marks statement block `\leanok` (correctly: "at least a sorry present"); proof block has no `\leanok` (correct: open obligation). No discrepancy with the blueprint's marking.
- `exists_tensorObj_inverse` at line 2210: `:= sorry`. Blueprint correctly marks statement block `\leanok`; proof body explicitly states "Infrastructure-blocked." Consistent.
- `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` at line 2256: `:= sorry`. Open obligation.

### Excuse-comments (acceptable — documented open obligations, not active-code comments)

The comment blocks in the Lean file (e.g., lines 2118–2162 "iter-230 C-wiring diagnostic") are diagnostic notes recording probe outcomes, not excusing wrong production code. They are within a section that explicitly states "The diagnostic def is intentionally NOT committed." These are appropriate engineering documentation.

None of these are suspicious; all are documented helpers.

- **Coverage**: 26/30 `\lean{...}`-pinned declarations exist in Lean. The 4 missing ones (`dual_isLocallyTrivial`, `homOfLocalCompat`, `pullback_tensorObj_iso`, `tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid`) are open proof obligations correctly marked as unformalized (no `\leanok`).

The `% NOTE` block at lines 2913–2936 should be reconciled with the updated proof body.
